target.title: fix(readme): hide default clawtributor avatars
target.body:
## Summary

- Hide two default-avatar clawtributors from the visible README wall so their 420x420 GitHub default PNGs no longer render larger than normal avatars.
- Keep the existing generated README convention by adding them to the hidden default-avatar cache instead of introducing hand-sized image HTML.
- AI-assisted: prepared with Nex; I verified the diff and proof output locally.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: README clawtributors entries for `constansino` and `18-RAJAT` rendered oversized because their GitHub default avatar URLs return 420x420 PNGs despite the `s=48` query parameter.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on Linux, rebased onto current `openclaw/openclaw:main`, with the patched `README.md` from this PR branch.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a local proof script that fetched the two reported avatar URLs, read PNG dimensions from the returned bytes, then parsed the patched README clawtributors block to confirm the oversized avatar URLs/profiles were absent from the visible wall and the normalized logins were present in the hidden default-avatar cache.

Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the local proof script:

```text
constansino: avatar image/png 1516 bytes 420x420
constansino: visible-url-present=False visible-profile-present=False hidden-present=True
18-rajat: avatar image/png 1560 bytes 420x420
18-rajat: visible-url-present=False visible-profile-present=False hidden-present=True
proof: PASS
```

Observed result after fix: The two target 420x420 default-avatar URLs are no longer present in the visible README clawtributors wall, and `18-rajat` / `constansino` are present in the hidden default-avatar cache that the existing generator convention uses for default avatars.

What was not tested: None

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #83628
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: the two reported clawtributor entries use GitHub default avatar URLs that ignore the requested `s=48` size and return 420x420 PNGs. The generated README Markdown image syntax does not set explicit width/height, so those default avatars can render oversized compared with normal 48px avatars.
- Missing detection / guardrail: these two logins were not present in the existing `clawtributors:hidden` default-avatar cache.
- Contributing context: `scripts/update-clawtributors.ts` already has a default-avatar hiding convention, so this patch follows that generated README shape instead of adding hand-sized HTML or binary avatar assets.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [x] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `README.md` generated clawtributors block and hidden default-avatar cache.
- Scenario the test should lock in: default-avatar accounts that return oversized default PNGs should not remain visible in the clawtributors wall.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: README-only generated-output update; the repo already stores hidden default-avatar logins in README metadata.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): `scripts/update-clawtributors.ts` has the default-avatar filtering convention; this patch updates the generated README state.
- If no new test is added, why not: README-only data/cache update, no runtime code path changes.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

The README clawtributors wall no longer includes the two oversized default avatars reported in #83628.

## Diagram (if applicable)

N/A

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: local checkout, README-only change
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): GitHub README rendering
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

1. Inspect the two reported clawtributor avatar URLs from current README.
2. Check their returned image dimensions.
3. Verify the patched README no longer contains those target URLs/profiles in the visible clawtributors block and does contain the normalized logins in the hidden default-avatar cache.
4. Run README formatting and whitespace checks.

### Expected

- The two oversized default-avatar entries are absent from the visible clawtributors wall.
- Their normalized logins are present in the hidden default-avatar cache.
- README formatting checks pass.

### Actual

```text
constansino: avatar image/png 1516 bytes 420x420
constansino: visible-url-present=False visible-profile-present=False hidden-present=True
18-rajat: avatar image/png 1560 bytes 420x420
18-rajat: visible-url-present=False visible-profile-present=False hidden-present=True
proof: PASS
```

Validation:

```text
git diff --check README.md
# no output

pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec oxfmt --check README.md
Checking formatting...
All matched files use the correct format.
Finished in 214ms on 1 files using 8 threads.
```

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Before evidence: the target `s=48` avatar URLs return 420x420 PNGs. After evidence: those URLs/profiles are absent from the visible README wall and their logins are in the hidden cache.

## Human Verification (required)

What I personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - #83628 is open.
  - No open PR overlapped `83628` immediately before opening this PR.
  - Branch contains one commit on top of current `upstream/main` and changes only `README.md`.
  - The two reported avatar URLs return 420x420 PNGs despite `s=48`.
  - The patched README hides `constansino` and `18-rajat` using the existing hidden-cache convention.
- Edge cases checked:
  - Did not use the existing `fix/clawtributors-avatar-size` branch because its README diff includes unrelated channel/runtime text and broad clawtributors regeneration.
  - Did not add binary avatar assets or switch to hand-sized HTML.
- What I did **not** verify:
  - I did not upload a rendered screenshot; verification is the README diff plus avatar dimension/visibility proof above.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: the clawtributors wall may be regenerated later.
  - Mitigation: the two logins are added to the existing hidden default-avatar cache that the generator already respects.
- Risk: maintainers prefer explicit sizing over hiding.
  - Mitigation: this PR follows the existing README hidden-cache convention for default avatars and keeps the diff minimal.
